creator: _jkrow
caption:
Save this for your Upstate NY bucket list if you only have a few days of PTO and want a winter trip that’s completely different from anything else in the Northeast ⛷️⛸️ 

@lakeplacidadk is the only place in the world where you can try the Olympic luge, learn to figure skate, ride the skeleton, ice skate on the world’s only remaining outdoor olympic speed skating oval AND visit four active olympic training sites. 

There is so much more you can do here and we already can’t wait to go back!

KNOW BEFORE YOU GO:

*This itinerary is best done Friday - Sunday in the winter so everything is open!

* Save money on admission to Olympic sites with the Olympic Legacy Sites Passport 

🏅 Say ‘Lake Placid’ and I’ll send you our exact Lake Placid Olympic Itinerary! 

#perfectdayadk #adirondacks

transcription.text: most people don't realize the most unique olympic winter town is in upstate new york. lake placid is the only place where you can actually do winter olympic events, visit four active olympic training sites, hike the best trails in the northeast, and do some great shopping all in the same weekend. and here's exactly how to spend three days exploring. on day one, arrive and head straight to the olympic luge complex. this is the only place in the u.s where you can actually try the luge for yourself and it's great for all ages. then check into your cozy hotel, grab dinner, and try the famous ubu ale at lake placid pub and brewery. and the night ice skating under the lights on the world's only remaining outdoor olympic speed skating oval. on day two, head to mount van hovenberg and then throw yourself headfirst down an ice chute. yep, this is one of the only places in the world where you can actually ride the skeleton without being an olympic athlete. then stick around for some snowshoeing or cross-country skiing. on your last day, ride the sky ride gondola all the way up to the top of the olympic ski jumps. if you're lucky, you might even get to see the athletes training here. before you head home, squeeze in a snowy hike, go dog sledding around mirror lake, or check out all the cute little shops along main street. say lake placid in the comments and i'll send you the exact itinerary.
